content.body:
Simple Interest:
I = PRT/100
A = P + I = P(1 + RT/100)
P=principal, R=rate%, T=time(years)

Compound Interest:
A = P(1 + r/n)^(nt)
r=annual rate (decimal), n=times compounded/year, t=years
Annually: A = P(1+r)^t

content.title: Compound Interest
content.body:
P=1,000,000, r=10%, compounded annually, t=3
A = 1,000,000×(1.1)³ = 1,000,000×1.331 = 1,331,000 UGX
Interest = 331,000 UGX (more than simple interest of 300,000)

questions.question: Simple interest on 200,000 at 5% for 2 years
Compound interest formula A=P(1+r)^t. P=1000, r=0.1, t=2. A=
Compound beats simple interest because
questions.explanation: In compound interest, previous interest is added to principal so next period earns more

questions.question: P=500, R=8%, T=4 years. Simple interest =
Effective annual rate when 12% compounded monthly
Amount after 5 years: P=2000, simple 6% per annum
Compound interest: P=5000, 10% annual, 3 years. Amount =
Rule of 72: years to double = 72/rate. At 8%, doubles in
questions.explanation: 72/8=9 years

questions.question: Which is better: 12% simple or 10% compound for 5 years (P=1000)?
questions.explanation: Simple: 1+0.12×5=1.6. Compound: 1.1^5=1.611. For 5 years compound is slightly better!

questions.question: P=10000, 15% simple interest. Years to double =
What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

content.body:
Profit = Selling Price - Cost Price
Loss = Cost Price - Selling Price
Profit% = (Profit/CP) × 100
Loss% = (Loss/CP) × 100
Discount = Original Price × rate%
VAT = Price before VAT × VAT rate
Income tax: applied to taxable income using tax bands

content.title: Profit and Loss
content.body:
Bought for 80,000, sold for 100,000
Profit = 20,000 UGX
Profit% = 20000/80000 × 100 = 25%

Sell at 20% profit: SP = CP × 1.2
SP = 80000 × 1.2 = 96,000 UGX

content.title: VAT and Discount
content.body:
Item costs 50,000. 18% VAT applied:
VAT = 50000 × 0.18 = 9,000
Total = 59,000 UGX

30% discount on 150,000:
Discount = 45,000
Sale price = 105,000 UGX

Cost price 50,000. 20% profit selling price =
Selling price 90,000. 10% profit. Cost price =
25% discount on 120,000 =
VAT 18% on item priced 200,000 before VAT =
Loss of 15% on cost price 80,000. Selling price =
Total price after 18% VAT on 300,000 =
Markup is calculated on
questions.explanation: Markup (profit%) is always calculated as percentage of cost price

questions.question: Income of 800,000 UGX. First 500,000 tax-free, rest taxed 20%. Tax =
Gross profit = revenue - cost of goods. Net profit =
questions.explanation: Net profit subtracts all operating expenses from gross profit

content.body:
Currency exchange:
Amount in new currency = Amount in original × exchange rate

Hire purchase:
Deposit + (monthly payment × number of months)
Total HP cost > cash price (paying extra for credit)
Extra cost = HP total - cash price

Exchange rates always given as 1 unit of one currency = X of another

questions.question: 1 USD = 3600 UGX. Convert 250 USD to UGX
1 GBP = 4800 UGX. Convert 600,000 UGX to GBP
HP: deposit 50,000 + 12 payments of 15,000. Total HP cost =
Cash price 400,000. HP total 480,000. Extra cost =
Why is HP more expensive than cash?
options: Interest charged for buying on credit
questions.answer: Interest charged for buying on credit
questions.explanation: HP spreads payments but adds interest/finance charges, making total cost higher

Rate: 1 KES = 28 UGX. 5000 KES =

questions.explanation: More UGX needed per USD means UGX is worth less (depreciated)

content.body: In this lesson, we explore advanced aspects of Financial Maths. Build on what you have already learned and apply your knowledge to more challenging problems.
Learning Objectives

By the end of this lesson you should be able to: (1) Apply Financial Maths concepts to complex problems. (2) Use appropriate formulas and methods. (3) Interpret results in context. (4) Connect this topic to related areas of mathematics.
Core Theory

This section of Financial Maths builds on foundational knowledge. In mathematics, advanced problem-solving requires not just knowing formulas but understanding when and how to apply them. Study each worked example carefully.

content.title: Worked Example 4.1
content.body: Consider a more complex problem on Financial Maths. Approach: (1) Read and understand the problem. (2) Identify all given information. (3) Determine which concepts apply. (4) Solve systematically, showing all steps. (5) Check your answer is reasonable.

content.title: Worked Example 4.2
content.body: Another type of problem you may encounter: Questions may test your ability to combine multiple concepts within Financial Maths. Practice identifying the type of problem and the appropriate method before attempting to solve.
Common examination questions on Financial Maths include: definitions and explanations, numerical calculations, diagram interpretation, and analysis of given data. Make sure you can handle all types.
Summary and Review

You have now covered all lessons in Financial Maths. Review the key concepts from each lesson, practice past paper questions, and ensure you can apply your knowledge confidently. Good luck in your studies!

questions.question: What does simplifying an expression mean?
options: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.answer: Reducing it to its simplest equivalent form
questions.explanation: Simplification means rewriting an expression in its simplest equivalent form by collecting like terms and applying arithmetic.

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a variable?
questions.explanation: A variable is a letter (like x, y, n) that represents an unknown or changing value.

questions.question: What is 2x + 3x simplified?
questions.explanation: Collect like terms: 2x + 3x = 5x (both terms have the same variable x).

questions.question: Solve for x: x + 7 = 15
questions.explanation: Subtract 7 from both sides: x = 15 - 7 = 8.

questions.question: What is the result of expanding 4(y - 3)?
questions.explanation: Distribute: 4 × y - 4 × 3 = 4y - 12.

questions.question: Which operation reverses addition?
questions.explanation: Subtraction is the inverse (reverse) of addition.

questions.question: What is 3² equal to?
questions.explanation: 3² = 3 × 3 = 9. The exponent 2 means multiply the base by itself.

questions.question: Factorise: 4x + 8
questions.explanation: The HCF of 4x and 8 is 4. So 4x + 8 = 4(x + 2).

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of y = 3x + 5?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, c is the y-intercept. Here c = 5.

questions.question: Solve: 3x = 18
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3: x = 18 ÷ 3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area of a rectangle = length × width.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a like term with 3x²?
questions.explanation: Like terms must have the same variable AND the same power. 3x has power 1, not 2, so it is not a like term with 3x².

questions.question: What is the gradient/slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so gradient = rise/run = 0/run = 0.

questions.question: If a = 4 and b = 3, find 2a + b.
questions.explanation: Substitute: 2(4) + 3 = 8 + 3 = 11.

questions.question: What does the inequality x > 5 mean?
questions.explanation: The > symbol means 'greater than'. x > 5 means x takes values greater than (but not equal to) 5.

questions.question: Solve: 2(x + 3) = 14
questions.explanation: Expand: 2x + 6 = 14. Subtract 6: 2x = 8. Divide: x = 4.

questions.question: The HCF of 12 and 18 is:
questions.explanation: Factors of 12: 1,2,3,4,6,12. Factors of 18: 1,2,3,6,9,18. Highest common factor = 6.

questions.question: What is the LCM of 4 and 6?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 4: 4,8,12,... Multiples of 6: 6,12,... Lowest common multiple = 12.

questions.question: Which number is prime?
questions.explanation: A prime number has exactly two factors: 1 and itself. 7 is prime (factors: 1 and 7 only).

questions.question: What is 0.5 as a fraction?
